articles.title: Ask HN: Can AI free us from horrible checkbox feedback forms?
articles.summary: I don't often fill in feedback forms because they never allow a proper answer. I did one recently for a service I received for free and felt obliged to give back. In one question there were about 15 options. Not one applied.Please can we have "How did you feel about this service?", with follow up questions if needed. If it fills in the same form behind the scenes I don't care. Just let me express what I need to say.I am not a great AI fan, but this would genuinely make life better. Comments URL:

articles.title: Show HN: Llm.sql – Run a 640MB LLM on SQLite, with 210MB peak RSS and 7.4 tok/s
articles.summary: Hi HN,I built llm.sql, an LLM inference framework that reimagines the LLM execution pipeline as a series of structured SQL queries atop SQLite.The motivation: Edge LLMs are getting better, but hardware remains a bottleneck, especially RAM (size and bandwidth).When available memory is less than the model size and KV cache, the OS incurs page faults and swaps pages using LRU-like strategies, resulting in throughput degradation that's hard to notice and even harder to debug.
